title: Wire isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget into bee worktree new (D1a)
action: Per D1(a), in handleWorktreeNew (canonical source: skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs -- guards.mjs's cell wcg-1 found this same mirroring architecture: 5 plugin trees + the vendored .bee/bin copy + the onboarding ledger; edit canonical files and propagate via scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs + skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply, repo-scoped only, no --global-skills, exactly as wcg-1 did): before creating the worktree, when flags['with-companion'] is not set, check whether isConcurrentMode(mainRoot) is true AND the main checkout contains a companion-eligible shared nested target. The existing isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget(root, targetPath) in guards.mjs (per validating's dependency-correctness review) checks ONE specific target's containment -- it walks UP from a target, it cannot answer 'does anything shared exist anywhere in this tree', so worktree-new time genuinely needs a DIFFERENT, complementary primitive: a directory-scan variant (e.g. hasAnySharedNestedCheckout(root) or similar name, worker's choice) that walks the checkout looking for any companion-eligible shared target (reusing the same underlying helpers -- targetInsideVerifiedCompanionMount's marker logic, and a submodule-aware nested-.git scan -- rather than duplicating logic). Add this new exported function to guards.mjs (both canonical and vendored copies, propagated via the same regen chain) in THIS cell -- this is now an EXPECTED part of the cell's scope, not a maybe. If concurrent AND the new scan finds a companion-eligible shared target, refuse the worktree creation with a typed, zero-mutation error (matching the existing typed-refusal style already in handleWorktreeNew for invalid slug / bad base-ref / existing path) that names --with-companion as the fix. If no concurrency, or nothing shared/companion-eligible exists in the source checkout, proceed exactly as today (D6 -- zero behavior change). This cell is `behavior_change: true`.

truths: `bee worktree new` without --with-companion refuses when isConcurrentMode() is true AND the main checkout has a companion-eligible shared nested target
`bee worktree new` proceeds exactly as today when either condition is false (no concurrency, or nothing shared/companion-eligible present) -- D6 backward compat
`bee worktree new --with-companion` is never refused by this new check regardless of concurrency
Prove it red-first, same discipline as wcg-2: a test showing worktree new currently SUCCEEDS unguarded today (pre-wiring) when concurrent with a shared target present, then green after wiring

artifacts.substantive: handleWorktreeNew gains a pre-creation refusal check, typed and zero-mutation, matching the existing refusal style in this function
new red-before/green-after test rows covering the refusal when concurrent+shared-target, and the 3 pass-through cases (no concurrency; no shared target; --with-companion present)

key_links: handleWorktreeNew calls the shared detection primitive from guards.mjs before any git worktree add runs
prohibitions: Do not weaken or remove any existing handleWorktreeNew refusal (invalid slug, bad base-ref, existing path/branch/grant)
Do not refuse worktree creation when --with-companion is passed, regardless of concurrency

regen_obligation_ack: release_manifest.mjs --check stays dropped from verify for the same pre-existing, already-filed, unrelated file-mode-drift reason recorded on wcg-1. The action still runs release_manifest.mjs --write for real content-hash regen of the touched files.

trace.outcome: handleWorktreeNew now refuses (typed, zero-mutation, hard fail-closed, no override) when isConcurrentMode(mainRoot) is true AND the main checkout holds a companion-eligible shared nested checkout AND --with-companion is absent, directing to a FRESH 'bee worktree new --with-companion' (D1a/D3/D4). Added the directory-scan guards.mjs export hasAnySharedNestedCheckout (walk-DOWN companion to wcg-1's walk-UP point-check), reusing the same companion-marker verification (refactored into resolveVerifiedCompanionMountReal) and submodule-registration exclusion. --with-companion is never refused; solo or nothing-shared is a pure no-op (D6). Propagated all 6 mirrored copies via render + onboard --apply (repo-scoped).
trace.friction: The cell files listed only 6 bee.mjs + 6 guards.mjs + release-manifest + onboarding + test, but guards.mjs/bee.mjs are skill-template files, so the sanctioned render step also rewrote 4 .bee-render.json (.agents/.claude/.claude-plugin/.codex-plugin) — deterministic regen side-effects committed for tree consistency, same mirroring architecture wcg-1 flagged.
trace.verify_output: node --test: SUMMARY 15/15 passed (tests 1 / pass 1 / fail 0), incl. 4 new wcg-3 rows: refused+zero-mutation (case6), refusal names --with-companion (case6), solo+nested proceeds D6 (case7), concurrent+no-nested proceeds D6 (case8), concurrent+nested+--with-companion mounts unrefused (case9). ledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches onboarding managed-hash ledger. exit 0.
